steps.title: Identify the interrupting phrase
steps.content: The phrase “in the process” interrupts between the two coordinated actions “alter the particles” and “dismantle any information.” It's non-essential—it clarifies the relationship between the actions but could be removed without breaking the sentence.

steps.title: Apply the matching-punctuation rule
steps.content: An interrupting or parenthetical phrase must be enclosed by matching punctuation on both sides (commas, dashes, or parentheses). Here, “in the process” requires a comma both before and after it. Choice B provides both: “and, in the process,” The other choices fail this rule by missing either the opening comma (A, C) or the closing comma (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
